data.description:
## What
An OPTIONAL, isolated backfill adapter that mines this machine's Claude Code session transcripts to reconstruct the LOCAL env/integration-diagnosis history (and corroborate attempts/replanning/recovery), which no structural signal captures. Output is LLM-classified and clearly labeled low-confidence; the core command does not depend on this adapter.

## Files
- `src/rebar/metrics/adapters/__init__.py`, `src/rebar/metrics/adapters/claude_transcripts.py` — new isolated adapter (imported only by the backfill script, never by the core command).
- `scripts/backfill_transcripts.py` — new: targeted extraction (grep error signatures) -> classify -> write labeled snapshots.
- `tests/unit/metrics/test_transcript_adapter.py` — new.

## Approach
Because all development happened on one machine, the transcripts are a near-complete local record of env toil that leaves no structural trace. To keep the harness portable, the adapter is optional and quarantined behind the backfill script — a parallel adapter could target another harness. It is a targeted pass (error-signature grep first, then classify candidates), NOT a blind full-corpus LLM sweep, to bound cost. This LLM post-hoc classification is used only as a calibrated backfill (never a system of record). The classification enum (`env_setup`, `dependency`, `integration`, `tooling`, `none`) and the JSON output schema are defined in the adapter; a human-labeled calibration sub-sample bounds the false-positive rate; every emitted value carries `source=backfill_classified, confidence=classified` and is segregated from structural series.

## Tests
- Unit (fixture JSONL transcripts): a transcript with a known env-failure signature is extracted and classified into an `env_setup` interval; a clean transcript yields none; output carries the classified label.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/unit/metrics/test_transcript_adapter.py` passes: a fixture transcript with an env-failure signature yields at least one classified interval tagged `source=backfill_classified` (`jq -e`); a clean transcript yields 0.
- [ ] the portability guard holds: a unit test asserts `src/rebar/metrics/registry.py` and `src/rebar/_commands/metrics.py` do not import `adapters.claude_transcripts`.
- [ ] the classification enum and output schema are defined in the adapter module — test asserts the enum has the documented values.
data.title: Local Claude-Code-transcript mining adapter (optional, isolated backfill)
